entities.name: KRAS G12C
entities.context: KRAS G12C mutations in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) partially respond to KRAS G12C covalent inhibitors.

entities.name: MRAS:SHOC2:PP1C complex
entities.context: The high-resolution MRAS:SHOC2:PP1C crystallization structure allows in silico analysis for drug development.

entities.name: Scribble pathway
entities.context: Activation of MRAS occurs from alterations in the Scribble and Hippo-dependent pathways

entities.name: Hippo signaling pathway
entities.context: alterations in the Scribble and Hippo-dependent pathways, leading to YAP activation

entities.name: YAP
entities.context: alterations in the Scribble and Hippo-dependent pathways, leading to YAP activation

entities.name: STAT3
entities.context: Other mechanisms that involve STAT3 signaling are intertwined with the activation of MRAS.

entities.name: STAT3 signaling pathway
entities.context: Other mechanisms that involve STAT3 signaling are intertwined with the activation of MRAS.

entities.name: HUWE1
entities.context: Activation of MRAS:SHOC2:PP1C is primarily Scribble-driven and downregulated by HUWE1.

entities.name: VCP
entities.context: The reactivation of the MRAS complex is carried out by valosin containing protein (VCP).

entities.name: STK11
entities.context: Comutations in STK11/LKB1 often co-occur with KRAS G12C, jeopardizing the effect of immune checkpoint (anti-PD1/PDL1) inhibitors.

entities.context: early adaptive resistance occurs due to rewiring of signaling pathways, activating receptor tyrosine kinases
